title: ORIASC-HCB / OIC Halal Certification Body Accreditation for Saudi SFDA Recognition

oic_smiic_framework.description: The Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) halal system is governed by standards developed by the Standards and Metrology Institute for Islamic Countries (SMIIC).

key_standards.title: Conformity Assessment – Requirements for Bodies Providing Halal Certification
Conformity Assessment – Requirements for Halal Accreditation Bodies Accrediting Halal Conformity Assessment Bodies

oic_smiic_framework.purpose: Facilitate recognition and acceptance of halal certifications on a national, OIC, and international basis to support international trade.
oic_smiic_framework.smiic_cca: SMIIC Committee on Standards for Conformity Assessment (SMIIC/CCA) prepares policies and standards for testing, calibration, certification, inspection, and accreditation.

halal_certification_body_hcb.definition: A body that operates halal product, service, process, or management system certification in a competent, consistent, and impartial manner.
general_requirements: Take full responsibility for conformity with all Islamic requirements
Sign halal certification agreement with clients
Full responsibility for halal certification decisions
Use halal certification marks adhering to Islamic values
Maintain impartiality (avoid offering consultancy services to maintain objectivity)
Evaluate and minimize conflicts of interest
Ensure financial stability and liability coverage
Maintain transparent and non-discriminatory fee policies
Implement traceability with unique identifiers on products and packaging

resource_requirements.personnel: Competent management and personnel with appropriate knowledge in relevant areas.
resource_requirements.audit_team_composition: At least two personnel: (1) a technical auditor with lead auditor qualifications and at least 4 years of work experience (2 years in halal certification), and (2) an Islamic affairs expert with at least 2 years of work experience in the Islamic work area.
resource_requirements.islamic_work_ethics: All personnel must adhere to Islamic work ethics.
resource_requirements.ongoing_competence: Defined competence criteria and ongoing evaluation of personnel performance.

process_requirements.initial_audit_and_certification: Formal process for initial audit and certification decision.
process_requirements.surveillance: Ongoing surveillance activities to ensure continued conformity.
process_requirements.special_audits: Provisions for special audits as needed.
process_requirements.appeals_and_complaints: Formal handling procedures for appeals and complaints.
process_requirements.record_management: Management of records and changes affecting certification.

oriasc_hcb.full_name: Oromiya Region Islamic Affairs Supreme Council Department of Halal Certification Body (ORIASC-HCB)
accreditation: Saudi Food and Drug Authority (SFDA) Halal Center
oriasc_hcb.role: National halal certification body in Ethiopia. Inspects and certifies agro-food and consumer products in accordance with Sharia law and Gulf Standardization Organization (GSO) standards to facilitate exports to Saudi Arabia, UAE, and other Gulf markets.

key_personnel.name: Siraj Ibrahim
key_personnel.title: Head of Halal Certification Department
key_personnel.education: DVM, Veterinary Medicine, Addis Ababa University

oriasc_hcb.chairman: Sheikh Bedru Nuru Tolosa (spoke at 1st Global Halal Industry Summit 2024)

saudi_sfda_hcb_recognition.requirement: Foreign Halal Certification Bodies (HCBs) must be formally recognized and accredited by the SFDA Saudi Halal Center in the country of origin to issue certificates valid for Saudi imports.
certificates_required: Halal Facility Certificate (HFC) — issued through halal.gov.sa
Halal Shipment Certificate (HSC) — issued through halal.gov.sa
saudi_sfda_hcb_recognition.recognition_process: HCB must be listed as an accredited/recognized body by SFDA Saudi Halal Center. Exporters must use only recognized HCBs for halal certification of products destined for Saudi Arabia.

audit_requirements_for_hcbs.initial_audit: Full facility audit by audit team (technical auditor + Islamic affairs expert) to verify compliance with halal standards and metrology.
audit_requirements_for_hcbs.surveillance: Ongoing surveillance activities as per OIC/SMIIC 2 requirements; frequency not explicitly defined in available sources but typically annual or bi-annual.
audit_requirements_for_hcbs.recertification: Periodic recertification required to maintain HCB recognition.
audit_requirements_for_hcbs.special_audits: Triggered by complaints, changes in facility operations, or non-compliance findings.
audit_requirements_for_hcbs.complaint_handling: Formal complaint and appeal handling procedures required under OIC/SMIIC 2; HCB must maintain records and implement corrective actions.

training_fee.exam: Online exam via Google Form; 70% pass threshold for Successful Completion certificate

fees_for_hcb_accreditation.actual_hcb_accreditation_fees: NOT explicitly published in available sources. Fees likely negotiated with SFDA Saudi Halal Center and/or national accreditation body (e.g., EIAC).

saudi_ethiopia.status: Saudi-Ethiopian Business Council established under the Federation of Saudi Chambers (announced February 2026 per Capital Ethiopia). Focus on overcoming barriers to livestock and meat exports.

saudi_somaliland.status: Saudi Arabia requested Somaliland initiate fish exports to replace disrupted supplies from Yemen. Key condition: establishment of dedicated health certification laboratory in Somaliland.

gcc_recognition_eatsafe.note: Eatsafe (Ethiopian halal certifying company, established 2018) received GCC Standardization Organization halal accreditation in December 2020. This is a precedent for Ethiopian HCB recognition by Gulf accreditors.

precedents_for_oic_hcb_recognition_in_li: Eatsafe Certification PLC (Ethiopia) — accredited by Ethiopian National Accreditation Office; received GCC halal accreditation December 2020.
ORIASC-HCB — accredited by EIAC and SFDA Halal Center; currently certifies Ethiopian agro-food products for Gulf export.

Saudi Arabia recognizes foreign HCBs through SFDA Saudi Halal Center portal (halal.gov.sa); meat and poultry imports require halal certificates from recognized Islamic centers approved by Muslim World League (historically) or SFDA-designated HCBs (current framework).
ORIASC chairman spoke at 1st Global Halal Industry Summit 2024 on 'The Responsibility of Issuing Halal Certificate,' indicating active international engagement.
gaps: Exact SFDA-published list of recognized HCBs in Ethiopia/Somaliland (need to verify ORIASC-HCB current status on halal.gov.sa).
Specific annual surveillance audit frequency for HCBs under SFDA recognition (OIC/SMIIC 2 implies surveillance but does not specify cadence).
Published fee schedule for HCB accreditation by SFDA or EIAC (not found in public sources).
Detailed complaint resolution timelines and success rates for HCB appeals.
Formal bilateral agreement text between Saudi Arabia and Ethiopia specifically for halal certification mutual recognition (Saudi-Ethiopian Business Council exists but specific halal MOU not located).
ORIASC-HCB's exact audit capacity, number of certified auditors, and geographic coverage within Ethiopia.
Whether ORIASC-HCB is the only SFDA-recognized HCB in Ethiopia, or if Eatsafe and others also hold recognition.
